description: This figure illustrates the microstructural evolution of Ni-containing Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel under varied isothermal annealing temperatures, combining scanning electron microscopy (SEM) for micro-scale observation and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) for nano-scale characterization. Subfigures a and b show the 500℃ annealed microstructure, consisting of B2 stringer bands, γ + κ matrix, and grain boundary B2 (GB-B2) precipitates. Subfigures c and d present similar micro-scale structure after 700℃ annealing, with sustained B2 stringer bands and γ + κ phases. Subfigures e and f are TEM bright-field images of high-temperature (900-1050℃) annealed samples: e displays nano-disk B2 precipitates in α matrix, and f reveals cuboid κ carbides uniformly distributed in γ matrix. The results validate that annealing temperature dominates phase morphology, distribution and type, supporting the correlation between heat treatment parameters and microstructure of low-density steels; Ni-free steels follow a similar trend except forming needle-like κ carbides in α phase at low temperatures instead of B2 stringer bands.
